extraction.one_line_summary: TRENTIN Matteo won stage 5 sprint after late crash delayed many rivals, attacking from 200m marker following Bora-Hansgrohe leadout.
tactical_patterns: DILLIER Silvan swept all four KOM climbs from breakaway, securing classification before final stage—aggressive mountain jersey hunting pays dividends.
TRENTIN Matteo positioned behind Bora-Hansgrohe train with only one teammate, parasiting rival leadout when outnumbered reveals sprint efficiency.
Breakaway included both climbing specialists and TT powerhouses (Martin, Mullen), mixing strengths to maximize survival odds on varied terrain.

rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: dominated all four KOM climbs from break, strategically secured classification with stage remaining
rider_signals.TRENTIN Matteo: positive: timed sprint from 200m perfectly, strong enough to win 212km stage in European champion jersey
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: second place finish moved him into points lead, consistent positioning despite team doing leadout for others
